steps.title: Identify the initial condition
steps.content: The phrase when the beaker was placed on the table refers to the starting moment, which is t = 0 minutes. We need to find g(0).

steps.title: Substitute t = 0 and use the exponent rule
steps.content:
Substitute t = 0 into the function:

$$g(0) = 295 + (361 - 295)(2.72)^{-0.104(0)}$$

Since any nonzero number raised to the power 0 equals 1:

$$(2.72)^{0} = 1$$

The exponential term simplifies away, leaving:

$$g(0) = 295 + (361 - 295)(1) = 295 + 66 = 361$$

The approximate temperature when the beaker was placed on the table was 361 kelvins.
